Class TurnManager

java.lang.Object
it.polimi.ingsw.am25.server.model.Game.TurnManager
public class TurnManager extends Object
Manages the turn order for both the placing and playing phases. The placing order is determined by the positions on the default tiles; the playing order by the positions on the offer tiles.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    TurnManager(BoardView boardView)
    Creates a TurnManager backed by the given board view.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Player
    getNextPlacingPlayer()
    Returns the next placing player and removes them from the placing-order queue, automatically skipping any DISCONNECTED players until a connected one is found.
    Player
    getNextPlayingPlayer()
    Returns the next playing player and removes them from the playing-order queue, automatically skipping any DISCONNECTED players until a connected one is found.
    List<Player>
    getPlacingOrder()
    Returns the current (unmodified) placing-order queue.
    List<Player>
    getPlayingOrder()
    Returns the current (unmodified) playing-order queue.
    void
    reAddPlayer(Player player)
    Re-adds a reconnected player to the end of both turn queues, provided they are not already present.
    void
    removePlayer(Player player)
    Removes a disconnected player from both the placing and playing queues immediately.
    void
    updatePlacingOrder()
    Refreshes the placing order from the board by reading the current default-tile positions.
    void
    updatePlayingOrder()
    Refreshes the playing order from the board by reading the current offer-tile positions.

  • Constructor Details

    • TurnManager

      public TurnManager(BoardView boardView)
      Creates a TurnManager backed by the given board view.
      Parameters:
      boardView - the board view used to determine player positions

  • Method Details

    • getNextPlayingPlayer

      public Player getNextPlayingPlayer() throws EndOfPlayingPhaseException
      Returns the next playing player and removes them from the playing-order queue, automatically skipping any DISCONNECTED players until a connected one is found. When the queue is empty (or contains only disconnected players) all players have resolved their actions for this round.
      Returns:
      the next connected player to resolve their actions
      Throws:
      EndOfPlayingPhaseException - if there are no more connected players in the queue

    • getNextPlacingPlayer

      public Player getNextPlacingPlayer() throws EndOfPlacingPhaseException
      Returns the next placing player and removes them from the placing-order queue, automatically skipping any DISCONNECTED players until a connected one is found. When the queue is empty (or contains only disconnected players) all players have placed their totems for this round.
      Returns:
      the next connected player to place their totem
      Throws:
      EndOfPlacingPhaseException - if there are no more connected players in the queue

    • removePlayer

      public void removePlayer(Player player)
      Removes a disconnected player from both the placing and playing queues immediately. This prevents the game from waiting for a player who will never act.
      Parameters:
      player - the player to remove.

    • reAddPlayer

      public void reAddPlayer(Player player)
      Re-adds a reconnected player to the end of both turn queues, provided they are not already present.
      Parameters:
      player - the reconnected player.

    • getPlacingOrder

      public List<Player> getPlacingOrder()
      Returns the current (unmodified) placing-order queue.
      Returns:
      list of players yet to place, in order

    • getPlayingOrder

      public List<Player> getPlayingOrder()
      Returns the current (unmodified) playing-order queue.
      Returns:
      list of players yet to play, in order

    • updatePlayingOrder

      public void updatePlayingOrder()
      Refreshes the playing order from the board by reading the current offer-tile positions. Must be called at the start of every playing phase.

    • updatePlacingOrder

      public void updatePlacingOrder()
      Refreshes the placing order from the board by reading the current default-tile positions. Must be called at the start of every placing phase.
